payload.title: NHAI Project Saksham: Empowering Women through Skills
payload.short_content: Discover how NHAI's Project Saksham empowers women with skill development opportunities in infrastructure, fostering gender equality in the workforce.
payload.content:
NHAI Encourages Women Focused Skill Development through ‘Project Saksham’

In a significant move towards gender equality in the workforce, the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has launched Project Saksham. This initiative is designed to empower women by providing ample skill development opportunities in diverse sectors, particularly within infrastructure development.

Overview of Project Saksham

Project Saksham represents NHAI's commitment to enhancing the employability and entrepreneurial skills of women. This initiative is crucial for breaking barriers and promoting gender equality, especially in the traditionally male-dominated field of infrastructure. The project aligns closely with national skill development policies aiming to equip women with the necessary expertise for their personal and professional growth.

Importance of Skill Development for Women

Skill development is paramount for women as it not only enhances their employability but also boosts their confidence and independence. With women comprising a much lower percentage of the workforce in infrastructure sectors, initiatives like Project Saksham are essential for leveling the playing field. By investing in skill development, we can drive economic stability and empower women to contribute effectively to society.

Objectives of Project Saksham

The objectives of Project Saksham are multifaceted. They include:

  • Improving employability and entrepreneurship among women.
  • Aligning training programs with national skill development policies.
  • Promoting gender equality in the workforce, particularly in infrastructure sectors.
  • Training thousands of women by the year 2025.

Training Programs Offered

Under Project Saksham, NHAI has launched various training programs tailored for women. These programs focus on providing practical skills and theoretical knowledge to enhance their employment prospects. The training sessions are conducted in collaboration with various organizations to maximize their effectiveness and reach.

Impact on Women's Employment

The impact of Project Saksham on women's employment has been promising. According to initial feedback, participants have reported a high satisfaction rate and noticeable improvements in their job prospects. As the training continues to expand across various states, thousands of women are expected to benefit from enhanced skill sets that open doors to better employment opportunities.

Collaboration and Partnerships

Collaboration is at the heart of Project Saksham’s strategy. NHAI has partnered with numerous organizations to ensure that the training provided is relevant and beneficial. These partnerships not only enhance the project’s reach but also improve the quality of training, making sure that women are equipped with the necessary skills that match market demands.

Success Stories

Real-life success stories from the initial participants of Project Saksham highlight the project's success. Women who have completed training programs have reported securing better jobs and feeling empowered to take charge of their professional journeys. These success stories inspire others and are a testament to the positive change Project Saksham is creating in the community.

Key Takeaways

  • Project Saksham is a significant step towards gender equality in the workforce.
  • Skill development for women can lead to improved economic stability.
  • The initiative enhances the participation of women in traditionally male-dominated sectors.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is Project Saksham?

Project Saksham is an initiative by NHAI aimed at skill development for women in the infrastructure sector.

Who can participate in Project Saksham?

Project Saksham is designed specifically for women seeking to enhance their skills and employment opportunities.

Is there a fee to enroll in Project Saksham?

No, Project Saksham does not charge any fees for participants.

Conclusion

Project Saksham is more than just a skill development initiative; it's a movement towards empowering women and promoting gender equality in the workforce. By focusing on training and skill acquisition, NHAI is paving the way for women to take charge of their lives, enhance their economic stability, and contribute meaningfully to the infrastructure sector. For more information and updates about Project Saksham and how you can get involved, visit EFOS.

About the author: Dr. Akansha Jain is the Founder & Chairperson of the EFOS Foundation (efos.in). With a Ph.D. in Forex Risk Management, a Gold Medal in M.Com, and 18+ years of experience, she champions learn-and-earn pathways and employability-focused education for India's youth. Author of 'You Will Not Get a Job'.
